title: How to Host a Webinar to Generate Freelance Leads
summary: For many solo professionals, webinars are a high-risk marketing chore that attracts low-quality leads instead of valuable clients. This article provides a risk-mitigation framework that treats the webinar as a complete system, focusing on strategic audience targeting, professional automation, and content designed to build trust. The key outcome is transforming the webinar from a speculative gamble into a predictable engine that consistently attracts high-value, pre-qualified clients, saving time and safeguarding your reputation.
ai_overview_answer:
To create a successful lead-generation webinar, freelancers must strategically design it as a "trust engine" focused on attracting a small, ideal audience rather than a large, general one. This process begins by defining a "Minimum Viable Audience" (MVA) to ensure the content is magnetic to perfect-fit clients. The presentation itself should follow an "Understand, Solve, Trust" framework, first articulating the audience's problem with empathy before revealing a clear path to a solution. Instead of a hard sell, use a low-risk call-to-action, such as a free diagnostic call, to make the next step feel safe.

For solo operators, leveraging an "Automation Trinity" that connects the webinar platform, CRM, and email service provider is critical for projecting professionalism and managing follow-up. After the event, immediately segment attendees based on their engagement, such as the questions they asked. Send prompt, personalized outreach to the most qualified prospects within hours to convert interest into client conversations. This systematic approach turns a webinar from a marketing tactic into a predictable engine for acquiring high-value clients.
key_takeaways: Define a hyper-specific 'Minimum Viable Audience' to attract high-value clients and repel poorly-fit prospects before planning any content.
Replace high-pressure sales pitches with a 'risk-reversal' call-to-action, such as a free diagnostic call, to make the next step feel safe for clients.
Immediately segment attendees post-webinar and send a personalized, high-touch follow-up to your top prospects within hours to capitalize on peak relevance.
Measure webinar success not by attendee count but by qualified sales conversations booked, treating your own time as the primary investment cost.

author.name: Sofia Gonzalez
author.bio: A successful freelance creative director, Sofia provides insights for designers, writers, and artists. She covers topics like pricing creative work, protecting intellectual property, and building a powerful personal brand.

seo.meta_title: Host a Webinar for Leads & Attract High-Value Clients

@graph.name: How to Host a Webinar to Generate Freelance Leads
@graph.headline: How to Host a Webinar to Generate Freelance Leads
@graph.description: For many solo professionals, webinars are a high-risk marketing chore that attracts low-quality leads instead of valuable clients. This article provides a risk-mitigation framework that treats the webinar as a complete system, focusing on strategic audience targeting, professional automation, and content designed to build trust. The key outcome is transforming the webinar from a speculative gamble into a predictable engine that consistently attracts high-value, pre-qualified clients, saving time and safeguarding your reputation.

step.name: Your Strategic Blueprint — Design for Profit, Not Just Presence
step.text: The success of your webinar is determined by a series of strategic decisions made weeks before you go live. A flawless delivery of a flawed plan still fails. This is where you architect the event for a specific, profitable outcome. Forget the vanity metric of 500 registrants. For a solo professional, a massive, undefined audience is a liability. It forces you to dilute your message to appeal to everyone, which ultimately resonates with no one. Instead, your first and most critical task is to define your Minimum Viable Audience (MVA). This is the smallest, most specific group of ideal clients whose acute problem you can solve unequivocally. The most powerful lever you have for pre-qualifying leads is the price tag. The decision to host a free event versus charging a fee fundamentally changes the nature of your audience. A successful webinar isn’t measured by the number of email addresses you collect. It's measured by profitable client conversion. To understand your true return on investment, you must account for your most valuable asset: your time.

step.name: The Solo Operator’s Tech Stack — Project a 10-Person Team with a 1-Person Crew
step.text: With a profitable blueprint engineered for a specific ROI, your next point of leverage is technology. For a solo professional, the right tech stack isn't a cost center; it's the silent, automated partner that executes the work of an entire team. Your stack’s primary job is to eliminate risk and project an image of impeccable professionalism, ensuring the focus remains entirely on your expertise. The market is saturated with webinar platforms, but as a solo operator, your evaluation criteria are fundamentally different. You must prioritize features that directly mitigate the unique risks you face, such as reliability, automation, and branding. To create a truly hands-off system, you must connect three key pieces of technology: your webinar platform, your Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system, and your email service provider (ESP). Running a live event alone requires a ruthless focus on preparation to minimize real-time stress.

step.name: The Authority Performance — Engineer Trust and Demonstrate Expertise
step.text: Your webinar content has one primary job: to prove you are the safest, most expert choice to solve your audience's problem. This is about structuring your presentation to build trust, not just to transfer information. High-value clients aren't buying information; they're buying confidence. Structure your presentation to implicitly answer their three core questions: Do you understand my problem? Can you show me a path to solving it? Can I trust you to guide me? Your ideal clients are inherently risk-averse. A high-pressure, 'Buy Now!' CTA will shatter the trust you've built. Your call-to-action must feel like the next logical, safe step. Engagement tools are not for entertainment; they are for intelligence gathering. Every poll and Q&A session is a chance to better understand and segment your audience for effective follow-up.

step.name: The Post-Webinar Conversion System — Turn Attendees into Clients in 72 Hours
step.text: Hesitation is the enemy of conversion. While your competitors send a generic 'thanks for coming' email blast 24 hours later, you must execute a swift, precise, and personalized outreach plan. Your time is your most valuable asset. Do not treat all attendees equally. The moment the webinar ends, your sole focus is to segment and prioritize based on high-intent signals. High-touch outreach is not a sales pitch. It is a continuation of the value-driven conversation. Your goal is to demonstrate that you were listening intently. Finally, extend the life of your webinar by transforming it into a library of content marketing assets that address specific sales objections.

mainEntity.name: How do you price a webinar for lead generation?

acceptedAnswer.text: This decision is a powerful strategic filter. For most consultants aiming to generate leads, free is the correct price. It maximizes reach and lowers the barrier to entry, allowing you to capture the widest possible audience at the top of your funnel. However, a low-cost, paid workshop model ($50 to $150) can be a potent qualification tool. This isn't about revenue; it's about filtering for seriousness. A person willing to invest even a small amount has "skin in the game" and is demonstrating a higher level of commitment. This paid strategy is most effective when the topic is highly specialized and promises a specific, high-value outcome.

mainEntity.name: What is the best webinar platform for a solo consultant?

acceptedAnswer.text:
The "best" platform is the one that provides the most leverage and mitigates the most risk. Your decision should be anchored in three core principles:

1. Rock-Solid Reliability: Your professional reputation is on the line. Prioritize platforms with a proven track record of stability, as technical failures are the fastest way to erode trust.
2. Deep Automation: As a one-person team, you cannot afford to manage every detail manually. Look for platforms with robust, native automation for registration, reminders, and post-event follow-ups.
3. Seamless Integration: The ideal platform integrates smoothly with your primary email marketing service or CRM, ensuring that attendee data flows directly into your central system for sophisticated, automated follow-up.

mainEntity.name: How can one person run a professional webinar alone?

acceptedAnswer.text:
The key is to shift the work from the live event to the preparation phase, using automation as your co-pilot.

- Front-load Your Workflow: Pre-schedule every email and pre-load all your polls and on-screen offers directly into the webinar software before you open registration.
- Control the Flow: Announce at the start that you will have a dedicated Q&A block at the end. Answering questions from the chat in real-time divides your attention and can derail your presentation.
- De-Risk Your Tech: Use a high-quality external microphone for crisp audio and connect to the internet with a physical ethernet cable. A wired connection is vastly more stable than Wi-Fi and removes a significant point of failure.

mainEntity.name: How do you measure the ROI of a webinar for a service-based business?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tracking simple lead volume is a vanity metric. Your time is your inventory, so the only metric that matters is the acquisition of profitable clients. To get a true picture of ROI, track metrics that reflect lead quality, not just quantity. Key metrics include: Qualified Sales Conversations Booked, Lead-to-Client Conversion Rate, and Average Contract Value (ACV) of Webinar Clients.

mainEntity.name: What's the ideal length for a lead generation webinar?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Respect your audience's time. For busy professionals, the sweet spot is 45 to 60 minutes total. This is long enough to deliver significant value but short enough to hold their attention. Structure it with a clear agenda:

- 30-40 minutes of pure content: Focus on delivering your core framework. Your goal is to teach, not to sell.
- 10-15 minutes of dedicated Q&A: This is often the highest-value portion of the event, where you can address specific concerns and build rapport.

This structure maximizes engagement and ensures attendees leave feeling their time was well spent, making them more receptive to your follow-up.

mainEntity.name: How far in advance should you promote a webinar?

acceptedAnswer.text:
For busy professionals, a long promotion cycle is ineffective. A condensed, high-intensity period is far more effective. The ideal window is two weeks.

- 14 Days Out: Announce the webinar to your core audience (email list, social channels).
- 7-10 Days Out: Begin dedicated promotion, detailing the specific problems you will solve.
- The Final 48 Hours: This is when most registrations occur. Send "last chance" and "starting soon" reminders to create urgency and keep your event top-of-mind.

body.content: How to Build a Scalable Trust Engine: A Risk-Mitigation Framework for Generating High-Value Freelance Leads

Transforming your webinar from a marketing chore into a trust engine begins long before you consider technology or a title. It starts with a strategic blueprint, an architecture designed for profit, not just presence. This initial planning is your greatest point of leverage and your strongest defense against risk, ensuring every ounce of effort attracts clients who value your expertise, not prospects seeking a freebie. The goal is precision, not volume; we are here to attract the right few, not the curious many.
Module 1: Your Strategic Blueprint — Design for Profit, Not Just Presence

The success of your webinar is determined by a series of strategic decisions made weeks before you go live. A flawless delivery of a flawed plan still fails. This is where you architect the event for a specific, profitable outcome.
Define Your "Minimum Viable Audience" (MVA)

Forget the vanity metric of 500 registrants. For a solo professional, a massive, undefined audience is a liability. It forces you to dilute your message to appeal to everyone, which ultimately resonates with no one. Instead, your first and most critical task is to define your **Minimum Viable Audience (MVA)**. This is the smallest, most specific group of ideal clients whose acute problem you can solve unequivocally. Think laser beam, not floodlight.

items: **Instead of:** "Marketing managers."
**Target:** "Marketing managers at B2B fintech startups between Seed and Series B funding who are struggling to convert blog traffic into qualified demos."

body.content: This specificity makes your content magnetic to the right people; they feel understood before you say a word. It also acts as a powerful, passive filter, repelling prospects who are a bad fit and saving you countless hours of follow-up with tire-kickers.
The "Free Webinar vs. Paid Workshop" Decision Matrix

The most powerful lever you have for pre-qualifying leads is the price tag. The decision to host a free event versus charging a fee fundamentally changes the nature of your audience. This isn't just a pricing question; it's a strategic control lever.

rows: "I'm curious and looking for free information."
"I have a painful problem and I'm investing to solve it."
Uniformly high; attendees have self-identified as committed buyers.
Introducing a broad audience to a common problem you solve.
Teaching a specific, advanced skill to a niche audience.

body.content: Calculate Your True ROI (Beyond Lead Count)

A successful webinar isn’t measured by the number of email addresses you collect. It's measured by profitable client conversion. To understand your true return on investment, you must account for your most valuable asset: **your time.**
First, calculate your total investment:

items: **Time Cost:** Your billable hourly rate multiplied by the hours spent on content creation, promotion, and delivery. If you bill at $200/hour and spend 15 hours, that's a $3,000 investment.
**Hard Costs:** The monthly fees for your webinar platform, email marketing service, and any advertising spend.

body.content: Next, measure the return against metrics that matter:

items: **Qualified Sales Conversations Booked:** How many high-quality prospects booked a discovery call? This is a far more valuable metric than the total attendee count.
**Client Lifetime Value (LTV):** The ultimate measure. If one attendee becomes a long-term client, the ROI of that single webinar could dwarf its costs for years to come.

body.content: By shifting your focus from chasing volume to engineering value, you transform the webinar from a speculative marketing tactic into a predictable engine for attracting your most profitable clients. The engine that drives this operation is your technology.
Module 2: The Solo Operator’s Tech Stack — Project a 10-Person Team with a 1-Person Crew

With a profitable blueprint engineered for a specific ROI, your next point of leverage is technology. For a solo professional, the right tech stack isn't a cost center; it's the silent, automated partner that executes the work of an entire team. Your stack’s primary job is to eliminate risk and project an image of impeccable professionalism, ensuring the focus remains entirely on your expertise.
The Solo-Friendly Platform Analysis

The market is saturated with webinar platforms, but as a solo operator, your evaluation criteria are fundamentally different. You must prioritize features that directly mitigate the unique risks you face.

items: **Rock-Solid Reliability:** For a major corporation, a technical glitch is an annoyance handled by an IT team. For you, it’s a direct blow to your professional credibility. Prioritize established, browser-based platforms that don't require attendees to download software, as this simplifies access and reduces friction.

**Deep Automation Features:** Your time is your most valuable asset. The right platform works for you long after the live event ends. Seek out tools with built-in, automated email sequences for reminders and follow-ups, as well as on-demand access features that turn your presentation into a long-term lead generation asset.

**Professional Branding Capabilities:** You are your brand. Avoid platforms that trap you in their generic templates. The ability to customize registration pages, waiting rooms, and event materials with your own logo and colors is essential for projecting a polished, high-end image.

body.content: The "Automation Trinity" Workflow

To create a truly hands-off system, you must connect three key pieces of technology: your webinar platform, your Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system, and your email service provider (ESP). This "Automation Trinity" ensures a seamless flow of data that triggers the right action at the right time.

items: **Webinar Platform:** This is the front door. It captures registrant information and hosts the event.
**CRM:** This is your central brain. When a new person registers, the platform automatically sends their data to the CRM, creating a new contact record.

**Email Service Provider (ESP):** This is your communications arm. Triggered by the new CRM contact, the ESP begins sending pre-scheduled reminder emails. After the event, attendance and engagement data can trigger a highly targeted, segmented follow-up campaign from your ESP.

body.content: Setting up this workflow using native integrations or a tool like Zapier means the entire administrative side of your webinar is handled automatically, ensuring no lead is lost and every attendee receives a professional, timely follow-up.
The One-Person Live Event Battle Plan
Running a live event alone requires a ruthless focus on preparation to minimize real-time stress. This is your pre-flight procedure.

items: **T-60 Minutes: Tech Lockdown.** Connect via ethernet—never trust Wi-Fi. Close every unnecessary application and browser tab. Test your microphone and camera twice.
**T-15 Minutes: Open the Room.** Go live 15 minutes early. Display a branded welcome slide with a countdown timer and play background music to create a professional atmosphere.

**During the Event: Batch and Control.** Announce at the start that you will have dedicated Q&A blocks at the middle and end. This keeps you in control of the narrative and prevents you from getting derailed. Use pre-loaded polls to drive engagement without needing to type questions live.

body.content: A flawless technical delivery is your foundation, but the structure of your content is what builds the house of trust. Once you’ve mitigated technical risks, your focus must shift to mitigating your prospect's primary emotional risk: "Are you the right person to solve my problem?"
Module 3: The Authority Performance — Engineer Trust and Demonstrate Expertise

Your webinar content has one primary job: to prove you are the safest, most expert choice to solve your audience's problem. This is about structuring your presentation to build trust, not just to transfer information. Every slide, story, and talking point must be a deliberate step toward that goal.
The "Understand, Solve, Trust" Content Framework
High-value clients aren't buying information; they're buying confidence. Structure your presentation to implicitly answer their three core questions:

items: **1. "Do you deeply understand my specific problem?" (Empathy):** Start by articulating their problem better than they can. Describe the frustrating nuances, hidden costs, and emotional weight of inaction. This shows you've walked in their shoes and establishes a powerful foundation of empathy.

**2. "Can you show me a clear path to solving it?" (Competence):** Reveal your unique methodology or framework. You don't give away all the secrets, but you provide a clear, high-level roadmap. By showing them *how* you think, you replace their uncertainty with a sense of clarity and control.

**3. "Can I trust you to guide me?" (Authority):** Solidify trust with authentic stories and case studies. As Chief Creative Officer Timothy Kalinowski notes, a primary obstacle for businesses is that "they try to make it about themselves... and they lose the critical ingredient which is it has to be about your customer." Frame a real client story around their transformation, not your own brilliance.

body.content: Build Your "Risk-Reversal" Call-to-Action

Your ideal clients are inherently risk-averse. A high-pressure, "Buy Now!" CTA will shatter the trust you've built. Your call-to-action must feel like the next logical, safe step. It should be an offer of more value, not a demand for a sale.

rows: Provides immediate, tangible value and reinforces your expertise.
Frames the conversation as a problem-solving session, not a sales pitch.
Offers a bespoke piece of value that directly addresses their situation.

body.content: This approach respects their intelligence and positions you as a generous expert, making the decision to engage further feel both safe and smart.
Strategic Engagement: Use Polls and Q&A to Qualify
Engagement tools are not for entertainment; they are for intelligence gathering. Every poll and Q&A session is a chance to better understand and segment your audience for effective follow-up.

items: **Design Polls for Segmentation:** Don't ask, "Where are you tuning in from?" Instead, ask, "What's the single biggest barrier preventing you from solving [Problem X] right now? A) Budget, B) Lack of a clear strategy, C) Technical hurdles."

**Use Q&A to Identify High-Intent Prospects:** The questions people ask reveal their deepest concerns. When someone asks a detailed question about implementation, they are signaling a higher level of interest. This data is gold for your follow-up, allowing you to craft a personalized message that directly addresses their stated needs.

body.content: That intelligence you gathered is pure gold, but it’s worthless if it sits in the vault. The window of peak relevance after your webinar is brutally short.
Module 4: The Post-Webinar Conversion System — Turn Attendees into Clients in 72 Hours

Hesitation is the enemy of conversion. While your competitors send a generic "thanks for coming" email blast 24 hours later, you must execute a swift, precise, and personalized outreach plan. This isn’t just lead generation; it’s client activation.
The "Triage & Escalate" Follow-Up Strategy
Your time is your most valuable asset. Do not treat all attendees equally. The moment the webinar ends, your sole focus is to segment and prioritize.

items: **Score Your Attendees:** Immediately analyze the data. Your top-tier prospects are those who exhibited multiple high-intent signals. Did they ask a sophisticated question? Did their poll answers reveal a critical business pain? Does their title match your ideal client profile? Someone who asks, "How does this framework integrate with an existing ERP system?" is a far hotter lead than someone who asks, "Will I get a recording?"
**Segment Your Sequences:**
**The Top 10% (High-Touch):** These individuals receive a direct, personalized email from you within hours. This is a manual, high-effort process reserved only for the best-fit prospects.
**The Other 90% (Automated Nurture):** This group enters an automated, long-term nurture sequence that continues to provide value, keeping you top-of-mind until the timing is right for them.

body.content: Crafting the High-Touch Outreach Email

This is not a sales pitch. It is a continuation of the value-driven conversation. Your goal is to demonstrate that you were listening intently. A McKinsey survey found that a lack of speed in supplier interactions was the single biggest "pain point" for B2B decision-makers, mentioned twice as often as price. Speed, combined with personalization, is your competitive advantage.
Your email must:

items: **Lead with a Specific Reference:** Open by mentioning the exact question they asked or the poll they answered. (e.g., "Hi [Name], I was thinking about the excellent question you asked during today's webinar regarding [specific topic]...")
**Connect it to Their Business Problem:** Briefly show them you understand the *implication* of their question.

**Offer a Clear, Low-Risk Next Step:** Bridge directly to your "risk-reversal" call-to-action. (e.g., "...I'd be happy to schedule a brief, 15-minute diagnostic call to outline a few potential strategies for your specific situation.")

body.content: Repurposing with Intent
Finally, extend the life of your webinar by transforming it into a library of content marketing assets that address specific sales objections.

items: **Mine the Q&A for Blog Post Topics:** Did three people ask about budget implications? That's your next blog post: "How to Accurately Budget for [Your Solution]: A CFO's Guide."

**Create Objection-Handling Videos:** Take a tough, skeptical question you received and record a short, 2-minute video answering it comprehensively. This turns a single moment of live engagement into a powerful, evergreen asset you can send to future prospects who raise the same concern.

body.content: This systematic approach ensures the value you created becomes a compounding asset that continues to qualify leads, build authority, and close deals long after you've logged off.
Conclusion: Your System for Predictable Client Acquisition

This systematic approach transforms a webinar from a single event into a reliable, repeatable process for high-value client acquisition. You move beyond the world of unpredictable marketing tactics and are no longer chasing prospects, but attracting clients who arrive pre-qualified, pre-educated, and pre-sold on your unique expertise.

The real power of this framework lies in the control it gives you. Every poll that qualifies a lead, every automated email that nurtures a relationship, and every piece of repurposed content that handles an objection works in concert to protect your most valuable assets: your time and your reputation. You systematically eliminate the risk of engaging with poorly-fit clients, safeguarding your energy for the relationships that truly drive your business forward.

Ultimately, this is about moving from an operator who reacts to a strategist who directs. You gain the ability to forecast your growth with confidence because your lead generation is no longer a matter of luck or hustle. It's the predictable output of a well-engineered system. By manufacturing trust at scale, you invest your time only where it will yield the highest return, building a business that is not only more profitable but also more resilient and far more rewarding.
Frequently Asked Questions
How do you price a webinar for lead generation?

This decision is a powerful strategic filter. For most consultants aiming to generate leads, **free is the correct price.** It maximizes reach and lowers the barrier to entry, allowing you to capture the widest possible audience at the top of your funnel.

However, a low-cost, paid workshop model (**$50 to $150**) can be a potent qualification tool. This isn't about revenue; it's about filtering for seriousness. A person willing to invest even a small amount has "skin in the game" and is demonstrating a higher level of commitment. This paid strategy is most effective when the topic is highly specialized and promises a specific, high-value outcome.
What is the best webinar platform for a solo consultant?
The "best" platform is the one that provides the most leverage and mitigates the most risk. Your decision should be anchored in three core principles:

items: **Rock-Solid Reliability:** Your professional reputation is on the line. Prioritize platforms with a proven track record of stability, as technical failures are the fastest way to erode trust.
**Deep Automation:** As a one-person team, you cannot afford to manage every detail manually. Look for platforms with robust, native automation for registration, reminders, and post-event follow-ups.

**Seamless Integration:** The ideal platform integrates smoothly with your primary email marketing service or CRM, ensuring that attendee data flows directly into your central system for sophisticated, automated follow-up.

body.content: How can one person run a professional webinar alone?
The key is to shift the work from the live event to the preparation phase, using automation as your co-pilot.

items: **Front-load Your Workflow:** Pre-schedule every email and pre-load all your polls and on-screen offers directly into the webinar software before you open registration.

**Control the Flow:** Announce at the start that you will have a dedicated Q&A block at the end. Answering questions from the chat in real-time divides your attention and can derail your presentation.

**De-Risk Your Tech:** Use a high-quality external microphone for crisp audio and connect to the internet with a physical ethernet cable. A wired connection is vastly more stable than Wi-Fi and removes a significant point of failure.

body.content: How do you measure the ROI of a webinar for a service-based business?

Tracking simple lead volume is a vanity metric. Your time is your inventory, so the only metric that matters is the acquisition of profitable clients. To get a true picture of ROI, track metrics that reflect lead *quality*, not just quantity.

rows: The first true indicator of success. It measures how many attendees were compelled enough to take the next step.
Use a scheduling link as your primary call-to-action and track meetings booked from the attendee list.
Shows the effectiveness of your webinar and follow-up process in turning prospects into paying clients.
Divide the number of new clients by the total number of qualified sales conversations.
Average Contract Value (ACV) of Webinar Clients
Reveals the ultimate financial impact. Are your webinars attracting the high-value clients you're targeting?
Calculate the average initial project or retainer value for all clients sourced from the webinar.

body.content: What's the ideal length for a lead generation webinar?

Respect your audience's time. For busy professionals, the sweet spot is **45 to 60 minutes total**. This is long enough to deliver significant value but short enough to hold their attention. Structure it with a clear agenda:

items: **30-40 minutes of pure content:** Focus on delivering your core framework. Your goal is to teach, not to sell.
**10-15 minutes of dedicated Q&A:** This is often the highest-value portion of the event, where you can address specific concerns and build rapport.

body.content: This structure maximizes engagement and ensures attendees leave feeling their time was well spent, making them more receptive to your follow-up.
How far in advance should you promote a webinar?
For busy professionals, a long promotion cycle is ineffective. A condensed, high-intensity period is far more effective. The ideal window is **two weeks**.

items: **14 Days Out:** Announce the webinar to your core audience (email list, social channels).
**7-10 Days Out:** Begin dedicated promotion, detailing the specific problems you will solve.
**The Final 48 Hours:** This is when most registrations occur. Send "last chance" and "starting soon" reminders to create urgency and keep your event top-of-mind.
